{{Infobox historic site | name = Umami Kofun Cluster | native_name = 馬見古墳群 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|220px]] | image_map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}} | caption = Suyama Kofun in the Central Cluster (Special Historic Site) | type = Burial mound cluster (North Cluster, Central Cluster, South Cluster) | locmapin = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|39.2|N|135|44|16.7|E|region:JP-29|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture }} {{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = Umami Kofun Cluster
(Central Cluster) | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location of the Umami Kofun Cluster (Central Cluster)}} }} '''Umami Kofun Cluster''' (馬見古墳群) is a group of burial mounds located in the southwestern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, and {{ill|Yamatotakada|ja|大和高田市}} in Nara Prefecture. The cluster is situated along the Umami Hills and its surroundings and is divided into three main sections: the North, Central, and South Clusters. == Overview == The Umami Hills span approximately 7 km north-south and 3 km east-west at an altitude of 70–80 meters. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和・柳本古墳群}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The burial mounds were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant burial mounds during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|ja|佐味田宝塚古墳}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTUmami Kofun Cluster}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTUmami Kofun Cluster}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTUmami Kofun Cluster}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTUmami Kofun Cluster}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:馬見古墳群]]

{{Infobox historic site | name = Umami Kofun Cluster | native_name = 馬見古墳群 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|220px]] | image_map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}} | caption = Suyama Kofun in the Central Cluster (Special Historic Site) | type = Burial mound cluster (North Cluster, Central Cluster, South Cluster) | locmapin = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|39.2|N|135|44|16.7|E|region:JP-29|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture }} {{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = Umami Kofun Cluster
(Central Cluster) | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location of the Umami Kofun Cluster (Central Cluster)}} }} '''Umami Kofun Cluster''' (馬見古墳群) is a group of burial mounds located in the southwestern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, and {{ill|Yamatotakada|ja|大和高田市}} in Nara Prefecture. The cluster is situated along the Umami Hills and its surroundings and is divided into three main sections: the North, Central, and South Clusters. == Overview == The Umami Hills span approximately 7 km north-south and 3 km east-west at an altitude of 70–80 meters. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和・柳本古墳群}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The burial mounds were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant burial mounds during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|ja|佐味田宝塚古墳}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:馬見古墳群]]

ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:馬見古墳群]]

(Central Cluster) | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location of the Umami Kofun Cluster (Central Cluster)}} }} '''Umami Kofun Cluster''' (馬見古墳群) is a group of burial mounds located in the southwestern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, and {{ill|Yamatotakada|ja|大和高田市}} in Nara Prefecture. The cluster is situated along the Umami Hills and its surroundings and is divided into three main sections: the North, Central, and South Clusters. == Overview == The Umami Hills span approximately 7 km north-south and 3 km east-west at an altitude of 70–80 meters. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和・柳本古墳群}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The burial mounds were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant burial mounds during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|ja|佐味田宝塚古墳}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:馬見古墳群]]

{{Infobox historic site | name = Umami Kofun Cluster | native_name = 馬見古墳群 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|220px]] | image_map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}} | caption = Suyama Kofun in the Central Cluster (Special Historic Site) | type = Burial mound cluster (North Cluster, Central Cluster, South Cluster) | locmapin = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|39.2|N|135|44|16.7|E|region:JP-29|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture }} {{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = Umami Kofun Cluster
(Central Cluster) | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location of the Umami Kofun Cluster (Central Cluster)}} }} '''Umami Kofun Cluster''' (馬見古墳群) is a group of burial mounds located in the southwestern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, and {{ill|Yamatotakada|ja|大和高田市}} in Nara Prefecture. The cluster is situated along the Umami Hills and its surroundings and is divided into three main sections: the North, Central, and South Clusters. == Overview == The Umami Hills span approximately 7 km north-south and 3 km east-west at an altitude of 70–80 meters. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和・柳本古墳群}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The burial mounds were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant burial mounds during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|ja|佐味田宝塚古墳}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:馬見古墳群]]

{{Infobox historic site | name = Umami Kofun Cluster | native_name = 馬見古墳群 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|220px]] | image_map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}} | caption = Suyama Kofun in the Central Cluster (Special Historic Site) | type = Burial mound cluster (North Cluster, Central Cluster, South Cluster) | locmapin = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|39.2|N|135|44|16.7|E|region:JP-29|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture }} {{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = Umami Kofun Cluster
(Central Cluster) | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location of the Umami Kofun Cluster (Central Cluster)}} }} '''Umami Kofun Cluster''' (馬見古墳群) is a group of burial mounds located in the southwestern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, and {{ill|Yamatotakada|ja|大和高田市}} in Nara Prefecture. The cluster is situated along the Umami Hills and its surroundings and is divided into three main sections: the North, Central, and South Clusters. == Overview == The Umami Hills span approximately 7 km north-south and 3 km east-west at an altitude of 70–80 meters. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和・柳本古墳群}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The burial mounds were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant burial mounds during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|ja|佐味田宝塚古墳}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja:馬見古墳群]]

(Central Cluster) | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location of the Umami Kofun Cluster (Central Cluster)}} }} '''Umami Kofun Cluster''' (馬見古墳群) is a group of burial mounds located in the southwestern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, and {{ill|Yamatotakada|ja|大和高田市}} in Nara Prefecture. The cluster is situated along the Umami Hills and its surroundings and is divided into three main sections: the North, Central, and South Clusters. == Overview == The Umami Hills span approximately 7 km north-south and 3 km east-west at an altitude of 70–80 meters. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和・柳本古墳群}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The burial mounds were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant burial mounds during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|ja|佐味田宝塚古墳}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja:馬見古墳群]]

The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和・柳本古墳群}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The burial mounds were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant burial mounds during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|ja|佐味田宝塚古墳}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Umami Kofun Cluster-qq]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Umami Kofun Cluster-qq]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Umami Kofun Cluster-qq]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Umami Kofun Cluster-qq]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:馬見古墳群]]

{{Infobox historic site | name = Umami Kofun Cluster | native_name = 馬見古墳群 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|220px]] | image_map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}} | caption = Suyama Kofun in the Central Cluster (Special Historic Site) | type = Burial mound cluster (North Cluster, Central Cluster, South Cluster) | locmapin = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|39.2|N|135|44|16.7|E|region:JP-29|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture }} {{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = Umami Kofun Cluster
(Central Cluster) | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location of the Umami Kofun Cluster (Central Cluster)}} }} '''Umami Kofun Cluster''' (馬見古墳群) is a group of burial mounds located in the southwestern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, and {{ill|Yamatotakada|ja|大和高田市}} in Nara Prefecture. The cluster is situated along the Umami Hills and its surroundings and is divided into three main sections: the North, Central, and South Clusters. == Overview == The Umami Hills span approximately 7 km north-south and 3 km east-west at an altitude of 70–80 meters. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和・柳本古墳群}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The burial mounds were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant burial mounds during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|ja|佐味田宝塚古墳}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Umami Kofun Cluster-qq]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Umami Kofun Cluster-qq]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Umami Kofun Cluster-qq]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Umami Kofun Cluster-qq]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:馬見古墳群]]

Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和・柳本古墳群}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The burial mounds were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant burial mounds during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|ja|佐味田宝塚古墳}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. The region saw the construction of significant burial mounds during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|ja|佐味田宝塚古墳}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in (Central Cluster) | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location of the Umami Kofun Cluster (Central Cluster)}} }} '''Umami Kofun Cluster''' (馬見古墳群) is a group of burial mounds located in the southwestern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, and {{ill|Yamatotakada|ja|大和高田市}} in Nara Prefecture. The cluster is situated along the Umami Hills and its surroundings and is divided into three main sections: the North, Central, and South Clusters. == Overview == The Umami Hills span approximately 7 km north-south and 3 km east-west at an altitude of 70–80 meters. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和・柳本古墳群}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The burial mounds were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant burial mounds during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|ja|佐味田宝塚古墳}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation revision.comment: Text replacement - "巣山古墳}}" to "巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun}}"

{{Infobox historic site | name = Umami Kofun Cluster | native_name = 馬見古墳群 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|220px]] | image_map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}} | caption = Suyama Kofun in the Central Cluster (Special Historic Site) | type = Burial mound cluster (North Cluster, Central Cluster, South Cluster) | locmapin = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|39.2|N|135|44|16.7|E|region:JP-29|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture }} {{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = Umami Kofun Cluster
(Central Cluster) | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location of the Umami Kofun Cluster (Central Cluster)}} }} '''Umami Kofun Cluster''' (馬見古墳群) is a group of burial mounds located in the southwestern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, and {{ill|Yamatotakada|ja|大和高田市}} in Nara Prefecture. The cluster is situated along the Umami Hills and its surroundings and is divided into three main sections: the North, Central, and South Clusters. == Overview == The Umami Hills span approximately 7 km north-south and 3 km east-west at an altitude of 70–80 meters. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和・柳本古墳群}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The burial mounds were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant burial mounds during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|ja|佐味田宝塚古墳}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation {{Infobox historic site | name = Umami Kofun Cluster | native_name = 馬見古墳群 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|220px]] | image_map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}} | caption = Suyama Kofun in the Central Cluster (Special Historic Site) | type = Burial mound cluster (North Cluster, Central Cluster, South Cluster) | locmapin = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|39.2|N|135|44|16.7|E|region:JP-29|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture }} {{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = Umami Kofun Cluster

{{Infobox historic site | name = Umami Kofun Cluster | native_name = 馬見古墳群 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|220px]] | image_map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}} | caption = Suyama Kofun in the Central Cluster (Special Historic Site) | type = Burial mound cluster (North Cluster, Central Cluster, South Cluster) | locmapin = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|39.2|N|135|44|16.7|E|region:JP-29|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture }} {{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = Umami Kofun Cluster
(Central Cluster) | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location of the Umami Kofun Cluster (Central Cluster)}} }} '''Umami Kofun Cluster''' (馬見古墳群) is a group of burial mounds located in the southwestern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, and {{ill|Yamatotakada|ja|大和高田市}} in Nara Prefecture. The cluster is situated along the Umami Hills and its surroundings and is divided into three main sections: the North, Central, and South Clusters. == Overview == The Umami Hills span approximately 7 km north-south and 3 km east-west at an altitude of 70–80 meters. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和・柳本古墳群}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The burial mounds were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant burial mounds during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|ja|佐味田宝塚古墳}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Umami Kofun Cluster | native_name = 馬見古墳群 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|220px]] | image_map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}} | caption = Suyama Kofun in the Central Cluster (Special Historic Site) | type = Burial mound cluster (North Cluster, Central Cluster, South Cluster) | locmapin = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|39.2|N|135|44|16.7|E|region:JP-29|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture }} {{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = Umami Kofun Cluster
(Central Cluster) | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location of the Umami Kofun Cluster (Central Cluster)}} }} '''Umami Kofun Cluster''' (馬見古墳群) is a group of burial mounds located in the southwestern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, and {{ill|Yamatotakada|ja|大和高田市}} in Nara Prefecture. The cluster is situated along the Umami Hills and its surroundings and is divided into three main sections: the North, Central, and South Clusters. == Overview == The Umami Hills span approximately 7 km north-south and 3 km east-west at an altitude of 70–80 meters. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和・柳本古墳群}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The burial mounds were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant burial mounds during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|ja|佐味田宝塚古墳}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Kofun in Nara
