The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和・柳本古墳群|12=draft}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The Kofuns were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏|12=draft}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant Kofuns during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|12=draft}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳|12=draft}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|12=draft}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|ja|佐味田宝塚古墳|12=draft}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|12=draft}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳|12=draft}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=draft}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳|12=draft}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳|12=draft}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡|12=draft}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3|12=draft}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:馬見古墳群]] [[sn:mami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]

{{Infobox historic site | name = Umami Kofun Cluster | native_name = 馬見古墳群 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|220px]] | image_map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}} | caption = Suyama Kofun in the Central Cluster (Special Historic Site) | type = Kofun cluster (North Cluster, Central Cluster, South Cluster) | locmapin = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|39.2|N|135|44|16.7|E|region:JP-29|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|12=draft}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture }} {{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = Umami Kofun Cluster
(Central Cluster) | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location of the Umami Kofun Cluster (Central Cluster)}} }} '''Umami Kofun Cluster''' (馬見古墳群) is a group of Kofuns located in the southwestern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamatotakada|ja|大和高田市|12=draft}} in Nara Prefecture. The cluster is situated along the Umami Hills and its surroundings and is divided into three main sections: the North, Central, and South Clusters. == Overview == The Umami Hills span approximately 7 km north-south and 3 km east-west at an altitude of 70–80 meters. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和・柳本古墳群|12=draft}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The Kofuns were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏|12=draft}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant Kofuns during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|12=draft}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳|12=draft}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|12=draft}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|ja|佐味田宝塚古墳|12=draft}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|12=draft}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳|12=draft}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=draft}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳|12=draft}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳|12=draft}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡|12=draft}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3|12=draft}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:馬見古墳群]] [[sn:mami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和・柳本古墳群|12=draft}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The Kofuns were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏|12=draft}}, an influential aristocratic family. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|12=draft}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳|12=draft}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=draft}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳|12=draft}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳|12=draft}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡|12=draft}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3|12=draft}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:馬見古墳群]] [[sn:mami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Umami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] {{Kofun navbar}}

(Central Cluster) | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location of the Umami Kofun Cluster (Central Cluster)}} }} '''Umami Kofun Cluster''' (馬見古墳群) is a group of Kofuns located in the southwestern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamatotakada|ja|大和高田市|12=draft}} in Nara Prefecture. The cluster is situated along the Umami Hills and its surroundings and is divided into three main sections: the North, Central, and South Clusters. == Overview == The Umami Hills span approximately 7 km north-south and 3 km east-west at an altitude of 70–80 meters. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和・柳本古墳群|12=draft}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The Kofuns were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏|12=draft}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant Kofuns during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|12=draft}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳|12=draft}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|12=draft}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|ja|佐味田宝塚古墳|12=draft}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|12=draft}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳|12=draft}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=draft}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳|12=draft}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳|12=draft}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡|12=draft}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3|12=draft}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. (Central Cluster) | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location of the Umami Kofun Cluster (Central Cluster)}} }} '''Umami Kofun Cluster''' (馬見古墳群) is a group of Kofuns located in the southwestern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamatotakada|ja|大和高田市|12=draft}} in Nara Prefecture. The cluster is situated along the Umami Hills and its surroundings and is divided into three main sections: the North, Central, and South Clusters. == Overview == The Umami Hills span approximately 7 km north-south and 3 km east-west at an altitude of 70–80 meters. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和・柳本古墳群|12=draft}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The Kofuns were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏|12=draft}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant Kofuns during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|12=draft}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳|12=draft}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|12=draft}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|ja|佐味田宝塚古墳|12=draft}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|12=draft}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳|12=draft}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=draft}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳|12=draft}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳|12=draft}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡|12=draft}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3|12=draft}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} {{citation {{Infobox historic site | name = Umami Kofun Cluster | native_name = 馬見古墳群 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|220px]] | image_map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}} | caption = Suyama Kofun in the Central Cluster (Special Historic Site) | type = Kofun cluster (North Cluster, Central Cluster, South Cluster) | locmapin = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|39.2|N|135|44|16.7|E|region:JP-29|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|12=draft}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture }} {{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = Umami Kofun Cluster
(Central Cluster) | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location of the Umami Kofun Cluster (Central Cluster)}} }} '''Umami Kofun Cluster''' (馬見古墳群) is a group of Kofuns located in the southwestern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamatotakada|ja|大和高田市|12=draft}} in Nara Prefecture. The cluster is situated along the Umami Hills and its surroundings and is divided into three main sections: the North, Central, and South Clusters. == Overview == The Umami Hills span approximately 7 km north-south and 3 km east-west at an altitude of 70–80 meters. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和・柳本古墳群|12=draft}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The Kofuns were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏|12=draft}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant Kofuns during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|12=draft}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳|12=draft}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|12=draft}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|ja|佐味田宝塚古墳|12=draft}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|12=draft}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳|12=draft}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=draft}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳|12=draft}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳|12=draft}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡|12=draft}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3|12=draft}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3|12=draft|12=draft}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:馬見古墳群]] [[sn:mami Kofun Cluster]]

{{Infobox historic site | name = Umami Kofun Cluster | native_name = 馬見古墳群 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|220px]] | image_map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}} | caption = Suyama Kofun in the Central Cluster (Special Historic Site) | type = Kofun cluster (North Cluster, Central Cluster, South Cluster) | locmapin = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|39.2|N|135|44|16.7|E|region:JP-29|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture }} {{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = Umami Kofun Cluster
(Central Cluster) | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location of the Umami Kofun Cluster (Central Cluster)}} }} '''Umami Kofun Cluster''' (馬見古墳群) is a group of Kofuns located in the southwestern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamatotakada|ja|大和高田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Nara Prefecture. The cluster is situated along the Umami Hills and its surroundings and is divided into three main sections: the North, Central, and South Clusters. == Overview == The Umami Hills span approximately 7 km north-south and 3 km east-west at an altitude of 70–80 meters. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和・柳本古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The Kofuns were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant Kofuns during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|ja|佐味田宝塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:馬見古墳群]] [[sn:mami Kofun Cluster]]

revision.comment: 53 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:馬見古墳群 up to rev 98265326
revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = 馬見古墳群 | native_name = | native_language = | image = [[File:Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|220px]] | image_map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}} | caption =中央群の[[巣山古墳]]（[[特別史跡]]） | type =古墳群（北群、中央群、南群） | locmapin = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|39.2|N|135|44|16.7|E|region:JP-29|display=inline,title}} | location =[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[河合町]]・[[広陵町]]・[[大和高田市]] }} {{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = 馬見古墳群
（中央群） | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|馬見古墳群（中央群）位置}} }} '''馬見古墳群'''（うまみこふんぐん）は[[奈良盆地]]西南部の[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[河合町]]・同郡[[広陵町]]から[[香芝市]]・[[大和高田市]]にかけて広がる[[馬見丘陵]]とその周辺に造営された[[古墳群]]。県下でも有数の規模を持つ古墳分布地帯で、北群・中央群・南群の3群からなる。 == 概要 == 馬見丘陵は、盆地西部の南北約7キロメートル×東西約3キロメートルに広がる標高70～80メートルの[[丘陵]]地帯で、馬見古墳群はこの丘陵一帯に分布している[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm 公園の古墳-馬見古墳群と馬見丘陵公園]（奈良県サイト）。総数は250基以上を数え、[[佐紀盾列古墳群]]と[[大和・柳本古墳群]]とともに「大和3大古墳群」とも呼ばれる。 [[4世紀]]末から[[6世紀]]にかけて造営されたと見られる。古代豪族・[[葛城氏]]の墓域とみる説もある。この葛城地域には、古墳時代前期の中頃から有力な古墳の造営が始まり、前期中葉から中期には、墳丘長200メートルを超える規模の古墳が造営されている。 == 主な古墳 == 大型前方後円墳の分布により、大きく北群・中央群・南群の3群に分けられる。 === 北群 === *[[大塚山古墳 (河合町)|大塚山古墳]]（河合町川合、中期、前方後円墳、全長215メートル、後円部径118メートル、高さ15.8メートル、前方部幅123メートル高さ16.89メートル、墳丘は三段築成、葺き石と埴輪列をもつ。埋葬施設は[[竪穴式石室]]に[[長持形石棺]]ではと推測されている。） - 大塚山古墳群は馬見古墳群に含めない見解もある。古墳群は5世紀中葉と推定されている。 *[[島の山古墳]]（川西町唐院、前期、前方後円墳、190メートル） *高山塚1～4号古墳 **1号古墳（中良塚古墳、前方後円墳、全長68メートル、後円部径45メートル、高さ6.5メートル、周囲に平均14メートルの周濠、葺き石と円筒埴輪列（家形などの形象埴輪が含まれる）。 **2～4号古墳（3基とも円墳。1号墳の西南に点在している。） *九僧塚古墳（大塚山古墳のくびれ部に接する一辺33メートル、高さ3メートルの二段築成の[[方墳]]で[[陪塚]]） *丸山古墳（[[帆立貝形古墳]]、全長60メートル、周濠。） === 中央群 === [[画像:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|復元されたナガレ山古墳のくびれ部。前方部から見る。]] * *[[巣山古墳]]（広陵町三吉、前期、前方後円墳、204メートル）- [[特別史跡]]。日本を代表する周濠型[[前方後円墳]]。 *[[ナガレ山古墳]]（河合町佐味田）（[[5世紀]]前半、前方後円墳、105メートル）- [[史跡]]。墳丘の半分の[[葺石]]・埴輪列が復元される。 *[[佐味田宝塚古墳]]（河合町佐味田）- 史跡。30面を超える銅鏡が出土。その中に鏡背に四棟の家屋文様が描かれた[[家屋文鏡]]がある。[[王朝交替説#葛城王朝説|葛城王朝]]の存在を首肯する説においては、その末期頃の宮殿を描いたもので、それぞれの文様から高殿、高屋、高倉、殿舎であろうと推測されている。 *[[新木山古墳]]（広陵町新木山、中期、前方後円墳、200メートル） - 陵墓参考地 *[[乙女山古墳]]（北葛城郡河合町佐味田字乙女山・広陵町大字寺戸字乙女、[[帆立貝形古墳]]、130メートル、5世紀前半） === 南群 === *[[新山古墳]]（しんやま、広陵町大塚、前期、[[前方後方墳]]、137メートル）- 34面の[[銅鏡]]が出土。 *[[狐井城山古墳]]（きついしろやま、香芝市狐井・良福寺、後期、前方後円墳、全長109メートル、後円部径60メートル、高さ10メートル、前方部幅73メートル、葺石と[[円筒埴輪]]が施されている。周濠。） *[[築山古墳 (大和高田市)|築山古墳]]（つきやま、大和高田市築山、中期、前方後円墳、210メートル）- [[陵墓参考地]] *[[コンピラ山古墳]]（こんぴらやま、大和高田市築山、円墳、95メートル） *[[狐井塚古墳]]（きついづか、大和高田市池田、前方後円墳、75メートル） *[[池田遺跡]]（いけだいせき、大和高田市池田）-'''池田古墳群'''と呼ばれる埋没古墳11基を確認、[[形象埴輪]]などが出土。 **[[狐井塚古墳#陪塚|領家山1号墳]]（りょうけやま1ごう、大和高田市領家、円墳）- 池田遺跡に含まれる。また狐井塚古墳の[[陪塚]]（飛地ほ号）。[[埴輪]]などが出土。南側近接地で大型鶏形埴輪が出土[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館]] 2008年 pp.98-100。 ==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *泉森皎「大塚山古墳群」文化庁文化財保護部史跡研究会監修『図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3』同朋舎出版、1991年 ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 *奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館『[https://ci.nii.ac.jp/ncid/BA85609172 はにわ人と動物たち:大和の埴輪大集合:春季特別展]』2008年（特別展図録・奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館 第69冊） **「池田古墳群」pp.98-100 == 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[:Category:日本の古墳画像|日本の古墳画像一覧]] *[[馬見丘陵公園]] *[[古墳群]] == 外部リンク== *[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm 公園の古墳-馬見古墳群と馬見丘陵公園]（奈良県サイト） *{{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=大和の遺跡|date=20040609151831}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うまみこふんくん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]] [[Category:馬見古墳群|*]]

{{Infobox historic site | name = 馬見古墳群 | native_name = | native_language = | image = [[File:Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|220px]] | image_map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}} | caption =中央群の[[巣山古墳]]（[[特別史跡]]） | type =古墳群（北群、中央群、南群） | locmapin = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|39.2|N|135|44|16.7|E|region:JP-29|display=inline,title}} | location =[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[河合町]]・[[広陵町]]・[[大和高田市]] }} {{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = 馬見古墳群
（中央群） | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|馬見古墳群（中央群）位置}} }} '''馬見古墳群'''（うまみこふんぐん）は[[奈良盆地]]西南部の[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[河合町]]・同郡[[広陵町]]から[[香芝市]]・[[大和高田市]]にかけて広がる[[馬見丘陵]]とその周辺に造営された[[古墳群]]。県下でも有数の規模を持つ古墳分布地帯で、北群・中央群・南群の3群からなる。 == 概要 == 馬見丘陵は、盆地西部の南北約7キロメートル×東西約3キロメートルに広がる標高70～80メートルの[[丘陵]]地帯で、馬見古墳群はこの丘陵一帯に分布している[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm 公園の古墳-馬見古墳群と馬見丘陵公園]（奈良県サイト）。総数は250基以上を数え、[[佐紀盾列古墳群]]と[[大和・柳本古墳群]]とともに「大和3大古墳群」とも呼ばれる。 [[4世紀]]末から[[6世紀]]にかけて造営されたと見られる。古代豪族・[[葛城氏]]の墓域とみる説もある。この葛城地域には、古墳時代前期の中頃から有力な古墳の造営が始まり、前期中葉から中期には、墳丘長200メートルを超える規模の古墳が造営されている。 == 主な古墳 == 大型前方後円墳の分布により、大きく北群・中央群・南群の3群に分けられる。 === 北群 === *[[大塚山古墳 (河合町)|大塚山古墳]]（河合町川合、中期、前方後円墳、全長215メートル、後円部径118メートル、高さ15.8メートル、前方部幅123メートル高さ16.89メートル、墳丘は三段築成、葺き石と埴輪列をもつ。埋葬施設は[[竪穴式石室]]に[[長持形石棺]]ではと推測されている。） - 大塚山古墳群は馬見古墳群に含めない見解もある。古墳群は5世紀中葉と推定されている。 *[[島の山古墳]]（川西町唐院、前期、前方後円墳、190メートル） *高山塚1～4号古墳 **1号古墳（中良塚古墳、前方後円墳、全長68メートル、後円部径45メートル、高さ6.5メートル、周囲に平均14メートルの周濠、葺き石と円筒埴輪列（家形などの形象埴輪が含まれる）。 **2～4号古墳（3基とも円墳。1号墳の西南に点在している。） *九僧塚古墳（大塚山古墳のくびれ部に接する一辺33メートル、高さ3メートルの二段築成の[[方墳]]で[[陪塚]]） *丸山古墳（[[帆立貝形古墳]]、全長60メートル、周濠。） === 中央群 === [[画像:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|復元されたナガレ山古墳のくびれ部。前方部から見る。]] * *[[巣山古墳]]（広陵町三吉、前期、前方後円墳、204メートル）- [[特別史跡]]。日本を代表する周濠型[[前方後円墳]]。 *[[ナガレ山古墳]]（河合町佐味田）（[[5世紀]]前半、前方後円墳、105メートル）- [[史跡]]。墳丘の半分の[[葺石]]・埴輪列が復元される。 *[[佐味田宝塚古墳]]（河合町佐味田）- 史跡。30面を超える銅鏡が出土。その中に鏡背に四棟の家屋文様が描かれた[[家屋文鏡]]がある。[[王朝交替説#葛城王朝説|葛城王朝]]の存在を首肯する説においては、その末期頃の宮殿を描いたもので、それぞれの文様から高殿、高屋、高倉、殿舎であろうと推測されている。 *[[新木山古墳]]（広陵町新木山、中期、前方後円墳、200メートル） - 陵墓参考地 *[[乙女山古墳]]（北葛城郡河合町佐味田字乙女山・広陵町大字寺戸字乙女、[[帆立貝形古墳]]、130メートル、5世紀前半） === 南群 === *[[新山古墳]]（しんやま、広陵町大塚、前期、[[前方後方墳]]、137メートル）- 34面の[[銅鏡]]が出土。 *[[狐井城山古墳]]（きついしろやま、香芝市狐井・良福寺、後期、前方後円墳、全長109メートル、後円部径60メートル、高さ10メートル、前方部幅73メートル、葺石と[[円筒埴輪]]が施されている。周濠。） *[[築山古墳 (大和高田市)|築山古墳]]（つきやま、大和高田市築山、中期、前方後円墳、210メートル）- [[陵墓参考地]] *[[コンピラ山古墳]]（こんぴらやま、大和高田市築山、円墳、95メートル） *[[狐井塚古墳]]（きついづか、大和高田市池田、前方後円墳、75メートル） *[[池田遺跡]]（いけだいせき、大和高田市池田）-'''池田古墳群'''と呼ばれる埋没古墳11基を確認、[[形象埴輪]]などが出土。 **[[狐井塚古墳#陪塚|領家山1号墳]]（りょうけやま1ごう、大和高田市領家、円墳）- 池田遺跡に含まれる。また狐井塚古墳の[[陪塚]]（飛地ほ号）。[[埴輪]]などが出土。南側近接地で大型鶏形埴輪が出土[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館]] 2008年 pp.98-100。 ==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *泉森皎「大塚山古墳群」文化庁文化財保護部史跡研究会監修『図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3』同朋舎出版、1991年 ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 *奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館『[https://ci.nii.ac.jp/ncid/BA85609172 はにわ人と動物たち:大和の埴輪大集合:春季特別展]』2008年（特別展図録・奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館 第69冊） **「池田古墳群」pp.98-100 == 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[:Category:日本の古墳画像|日本の古墳画像一覧]] *[[馬見丘陵公園]] *[[古墳群]] == 外部リンク== *[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm 公園の古墳-馬見古墳群と馬見丘陵公園]（奈良県サイト） *{{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=大和の遺跡|date=20040609151831}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うまみこふんくん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]] [[Category:馬見古墳群|*]]

{{Infobox historic site | name = Umami Kofun Cluster | native_name = 馬見古墳群 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|220px]] | image_map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}} | caption = Suyama Kofun in the Central Cluster (Special Historic Site) | type = Kofun cluster (North Cluster, Central Cluster, South Cluster) | locmapin = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|39.2|N|135|44|16.7|E|region:JP-29|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture }} {{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = Umami Kofun Cluster
(Central Cluster) | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location of the Umami Kofun Cluster (Central Cluster)}} }} '''Umami Kofun Cluster''' (馬見古墳群) is a group of Kofuns located in the southwestern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamatotakada|ja|大和高田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Nara Prefecture. The cluster is situated along the Umami Hills and its surroundings and is divided into three main sections: the North, Central, and South Clusters. == Overview == The Umami Hills span approximately 7 km north-south and 3 km east-west at an altitude of 70–80 meters. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和・柳本古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The Kofuns were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant Kofuns during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|ja|佐味田宝塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:馬見古墳群]] [[sn:mami Kofun Cluster]] {{translated page|ja|馬見古墳群|version=98265326|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Infobox historic site | name = Umami Kofun Cluster | native_name = 馬見古墳群 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|220px]] | image_map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}} | caption = Suyama Kofun in the Central Cluster (Special Historic Site) | type = Kofun cluster (North Cluster, Central Cluster, South Cluster) | locmapin = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|39.2|N|135|44|16.7|E|region:JP-29|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture }} {{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = Umami Kofun Cluster
(Central Cluster) | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location of the Umami Kofun Cluster (Central Cluster)}} }} '''Umami Kofun Cluster''' (馬見古墳群) is a group of Kofuns located in the southwestern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamatotakada|ja|大和高田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Nara Prefecture. The cluster is situated along the Umami Hills and its surroundings and is divided into three main sections: the North, Central, and South Clusters. == Overview == The Umami Hills span approximately 7 km north-south and 3 km east-west at an altitude of 70–80 meters. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和・柳本古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The Kofuns were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant Kofuns during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|ja|佐味田宝塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{citation needed|Umami Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|馬見古墳群|version=98265326|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Umami Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:馬見古墳群]] [[sn:mami Kofun Cluster]]

The region saw the construction of significant Kofuns during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|ja|佐味田宝塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|馬見古墳群|version=98265326|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:馬見古墳群]] [[sn:mami Kofun Cluster]]

{{Infobox historic site | name = Umami Kofun Cluster | native_name = 馬見古墳群 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|220px]] | image_map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}} | caption = Suyama Kofun in the Central Cluster (Special Historic Site) | type = Kofun cluster (North Cluster, Central Cluster, South Cluster) | locmapin = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|39.2|N|135|44|16.7|E|region:JP-29|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture }} {{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = Umami Kofun Cluster
(Central Cluster) | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location of the Umami Kofun Cluster (Central Cluster)}} }} '''Umami Kofun Cluster''' (馬見古墳群) is a group of Kofuns located in the southwestern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamatotakada|ja|大和高田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Nara Prefecture. The cluster is situated along the Umami Hills and its surroundings and is divided into three main sections: the North, Central, and South Clusters. == Overview == The Umami Hills span approximately 7 km north-south and 3 km east-west at an altitude of 70–80 meters. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和・柳本古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The Kofuns were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant Kofuns during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|ja|佐味田宝塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|馬見古墳群|version=98265326|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:馬見古墳群]] [[sn:mami Kofun Cluster]]

The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和・柳本古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The Kofuns were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant Kofuns during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|ja|佐味田宝塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|馬見古墳群|version=98265326|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:馬見古墳群]] [[sn:mami Kofun Cluster]]

(Central Cluster) | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location of the Umami Kofun Cluster (Central Cluster)}} }} '''Umami Kofun Cluster''' (馬見古墳群) is a group of Kofuns located in the southwestern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Basin|qq=}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai, Nara|qq=}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|qq=}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiba|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yamatotakada|ja|大和高田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatotakada|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture. The cluster is situated along the Umami Hills and its surroundings and is divided into three main sections: the North, Central, and South Clusters. == Overview == The Umami Hills span approximately 7 km north-south and 3 km east-west at an altitude of 70–80 meters. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和・柳本古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Group|qq=}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The Kofuns were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi clan|qq=}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant Kofuns during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|qq=}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimanoyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Suyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|ja|佐味田宝塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samita Takarazuka Kofun|qq=}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomoyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpirayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Site|qq=}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|qq=}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991.
