[[ja:馬見古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287}}|part_of={{ill|Bakuya Kofun|WD=Q11570540}}; {{ill|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun|WD=Q17227934}}; {{ill|Nikiyama Kofun|WD=Q17210019}}; {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|WD=Q11369896}}; {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|WD=Q11383079}}; {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|WD=Q17213138}}; {{ill|Shin-yama Kofun|WD=Q11501998}}; {{ill|Suyama Kofun|WD=Q11479418}}; {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|WD=Q11603503}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11668062]] Wikidata name: Umami Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox historic site | name = Umami Kofun Cluster | native_name = 馬見古墳群 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|220px]] | image_map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}} | caption = Suyama Kofun in the Central Cluster (Special Historic Site) | type = Kofun cluster (North Cluster, Central Cluster, South Cluster) | locmapin = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|39.2|N|135|44|16.7|E|region:JP-29|display=inline,title}} | location = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]], Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture }} {{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = Umami Kofun Cluster
(Central Cluster) | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location of the Umami Kofun Cluster (Central Cluster)}} }} '''Umami Kofun Cluster''' (馬見古墳群) is a group of Kofuns located in the southwestern area of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], spanning [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]], [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kashiba, Nara|Kashiba]], and [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada]] in Nara Prefecture. The cluster is situated along the Umami Hills and its surroundings and is divided into three main sections: the North, Central, and South Clusters. == Overview == The Umami Hills span approximately 7 km north-south and 3 km east-west at an altitude of 70–80 meters. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和・柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The Kofuns were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the [[:en:Katsuragi clan|Katsuragi clan]], an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant Kofuns during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun|qq=}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * [[:en:Shimanoyama Kofun|Shimanoyama Kofun]] (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]] (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samita Takarazuka Kofun]] (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomoyama Kofun]] (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpira Yama Kofun|lt=Konpira Yama Kofun|lt=Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpirayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsuneizuka Kofun|lt=Kitsuneizuka Kofun|lt=Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Site|1=Ikeda Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552160}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]], "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Hill Park|qq=}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|馬見古墳群|version=98265326|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] }} [[ja:馬見古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] {{wikidata link|Q11668062}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11668062}}

(Central Cluster) | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location of the Umami Kofun Cluster (Central Cluster)}} }} '''Umami Kofun Cluster''' (馬見古墳群) is a group of Kofuns located in the southwestern area of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], spanning [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]], [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kashiba, Nara|Kashiba]], and [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada]] in Nara Prefecture. The cluster is situated along the Umami Hills and its surroundings and is divided into three main sections: the North, Central, and South Clusters. == Overview == The Umami Hills span approximately 7 km north-south and 3 km east-west at an altitude of 70–80 meters. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和・柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The Kofuns were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the [[:en:Katsuragi clan|Katsuragi clan]], an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant Kofuns during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun|qq=}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * [[:en:Shimanoyama Kofun|Shimanoyama Kofun]] (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]] (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samita Takarazuka Kofun]] (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomoyama Kofun]] (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpira Yama Kofun|lt=Konpira Yama Kofun|lt=Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpirayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsuneizuka Kofun|lt=Kitsuneizuka Kofun|lt=Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Site|1=Ikeda Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552160}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]], "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Hill Park|qq=}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|馬見古墳群|version=98265326|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] }} [[ja:馬見古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11668062}}

revision.text:
[[ja:馬見古墳群]] [[ja:馬見古墳群]] [[ja:馬見古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287}}|part_of={{ill|Bakuya Kofun|WD=Q11570540}}; {{ill|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun|WD=Q17227934}}; {{ill|Nikiyama Kofun|WD=Q17210019}}; {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|WD=Q11369896}}; {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|WD=Q11383079}}; {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|WD=Q17213138}}; {{ill|Shin-yama Kofun|WD=Q11501998}}; {{ill|Suyama Kofun|WD=Q11479418}}; {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|WD=Q11603503}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11668062]] Wikidata name: Umami Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox historic site | name = Umami Kofun Cluster | native_name = 馬見古墳群 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|220px]] | image_map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}} | caption = Suyama Kofun in the Central Cluster (Special Historic Site) | type = Kofun cluster (North Cluster, Central Cluster, South Cluster) | locmapin = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|39.2|N|135|44|16.7|E|region:JP-29|display=inline,title}} | location = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]], Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture }} {{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = Umami Kofun Cluster
(Central Cluster) | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location of the Umami Kofun Cluster (Central Cluster)}} }} '''Umami Kofun Cluster''' (馬見古墳群) is a group of Kofuns located in the southwestern area of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], spanning [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]], [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kashiba, Nara|Kashiba]], and [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada]] in Nara Prefecture. The cluster is situated along the Umami Hills and its surroundings and is divided into three main sections: the North, Central, and South Clusters. == Overview == The Umami Hills span approximately 7 km north-south and 3 km east-west at an altitude of 70–80 meters. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和・柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The Kofuns were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the [[:en:Katsuragi clan|Katsuragi clan]], an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant Kofuns during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun|qq=}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * [[:en:Shimanoyama Kofun|Shimanoyama Kofun]] (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]] (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samita Takarazuka Kofun]] (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomoyama Kofun]] (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpira Yama Kofun|lt=Konpira Yama Kofun|lt=Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpirayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsuneizuka Kofun|lt=Kitsuneizuka Kofun|lt=Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Site|1=Ikeda Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552160}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]], "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Hill Park|qq=}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|馬見古墳群|version=98265326|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:馬見古墳群]] {{wikidata link|Q11668062}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和・柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The Kofuns were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the [[:en:Katsuragi clan|Katsuragi clan]], an influential aristocratic family. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomoyama Kofun]] (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpira Yama Kofun|lt=Konpira Yama Kofun|lt=Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpirayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsuneizuka Kofun|lt=Kitsuneizuka Kofun|lt=Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Site|1=Ikeda Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552160}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]], "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Hill Park|qq=}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|馬見古墳群|version=98265326|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:馬見古墳群]] {{wikidata link|Q11668062}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:馬見古墳群]] [[ja:馬見古墳群]] [[ja:馬見古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287}}|part_of={{ill|Bakuya Kofun|WD=Q11570540}}; {{ill|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun|WD=Q17227934}}; {{ill|Nikiyama Kofun|WD=Q17210019}}; {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|WD=Q11369896}}; {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|WD=Q11383079}}; {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|WD=Q17213138}}; {{ill|Shin-yama Kofun|WD=Q11501998}}; {{ill|Suyama Kofun|WD=Q11479418}}; {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|WD=Q11603503}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11668062]] Wikidata name: Umami Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox historic site | name = Umami Kofun Cluster | native_name = 馬見古墳群 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|220px]] | image_map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}} | caption = Suyama Kofun in the Central Cluster (Special Historic Site) | type = Kofun cluster (North Cluster, Central Cluster, South Cluster) | locmapin = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|39.2|N|135|44|16.7|E|region:JP-29|display=inline,title}} | location = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]], Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture }} {{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = Umami Kofun Cluster
(Central Cluster) | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location of the Umami Kofun Cluster (Central Cluster)}} }} '''Umami Kofun Cluster''' (馬見古墳群) is a group of Kofuns located in the southwestern area of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], spanning [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]], [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kashiba, Nara|Kashiba]], and [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada]] in Nara Prefecture. The cluster is situated along the Umami Hills and its surroundings and is divided into three main sections: the North, Central, and South Clusters. == Overview == The Umami Hills span approximately 7 km north-south and 3 km east-west at an altitude of 70–80 meters. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和・柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The Kofuns were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the [[:en:Katsuragi clan|Katsuragi clan]], an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant Kofuns during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun|qq=}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * [[:en:Shimanoyama Kofun|Shimanoyama Kofun]] (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]] (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samita Takarazuka Kofun]] (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomoyama Kofun]] (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpira Yama Kofun|lt=Konpira Yama Kofun|lt=Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpirayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsuneizuka Kofun|lt=Kitsuneizuka Kofun|lt=Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Site|1=Ikeda Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552160}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. [[ja:馬見古墳群]] [[ja:馬見古墳群]] [[ja:馬見古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287}}|part_of={{ill|Bakuya Kofun|WD=Q11570540}}; {{ill|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun|WD=Q17227934}}; {{ill|Nikiyama Kofun|WD=Q17210019}}; {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|WD=Q11369896}}; {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|WD=Q11383079}}; {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|WD=Q17213138}}; {{ill|Shin-yama Kofun|WD=Q11501998}}; {{ill|Suyama Kofun|WD=Q11479418}}; {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|WD=Q11603503}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11668062]] Wikidata name: Umami Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox historic site | name = Umami Kofun Cluster | native_name = 馬見古墳群 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|220px]] | image_map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}} | caption = Suyama Kofun in the Central Cluster (Special Historic Site) | type = Kofun cluster (North Cluster, Central Cluster, South Cluster) | locmapin = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|39.2|N|135|44|16.7|E|region:JP-29|display=inline,title}} | location = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]], Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture }} {{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = Umami Kofun Cluster
(Central Cluster) | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location of the Umami Kofun Cluster (Central Cluster)}} }} '''Umami Kofun Cluster''' (馬見古墳群) is a group of Kofuns located in the southwestern area of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], spanning [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]], [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kashiba, Nara|Kashiba]], and [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada]] in Nara Prefecture. The cluster is situated along the Umami Hills and its surroundings and is divided into three main sections: the North, Central, and South Clusters. == Overview == The Umami Hills span approximately 7 km north-south and 3 km east-west at an altitude of 70–80 meters. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和・柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The Kofuns were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the [[:en:Katsuragi clan|Katsuragi clan]], an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant Kofuns during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun|qq=}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * [[:en:Shimanoyama Kofun|Shimanoyama Kofun]] (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]] (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * [[:en:Samida Takarazuka Kofun|Samita Takarazuka Kofun]] (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomoyama Kofun]] (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpira Yama Kofun|lt=Konpira Yama Kofun|lt=Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpirayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsuneizuka Kofun|lt=Kitsuneizuka Kofun|lt=Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Site|1=Ikeda Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552160}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]], "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Hill Park|qq=}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|馬見古墳群|version=98265326|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:馬見古墳群]] {{wikidata link|Q11668062}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和・柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The Kofuns were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|en|Katsuragi clan|WD=Q11620908|lt=Katsuragi clan}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant Kofuns during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun|qq=}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|en|Shimanoyama Kofun|WD=Q17213138|lt=Shimanoyama Kofun}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|en|Suyama Kofun|WD=Q11479418|lt=Suyama Kofun}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samida Takarazuka Kofun|en|Samida Takarazuka Kofun|WD=Q11383079|lt=Samita Takarazuka Kofun}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. [[ja:馬見古墳群]] [[ja:馬見古墳群]] [[ja:馬見古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287}}|part_of={{ill|Bakuya Kofun|WD=Q11570540}}; {{ill|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun|WD=Q17227934}}; {{ill|Nikiyama Kofun|WD=Q17210019}}; {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|WD=Q11369896}}; {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|WD=Q11383079}}; {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|WD=Q17213138}}; {{ill|Shin-yama Kofun|WD=Q11501998}}; {{ill|Suyama Kofun|WD=Q11479418}}; {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|WD=Q11603503}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11668062]] Wikidata name: Umami Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox historic site | name = Umami Kofun Cluster | native_name = 馬見古墳群 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|220px]] | image_map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}} | caption = Suyama Kofun in the Central Cluster (Special Historic Site) | type = Kofun cluster (North Cluster, Central Cluster, South Cluster) | locmapin = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|39.2|N|135|44|16.7|E|region:JP-29|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt=Kōryō, Nara}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|en|Kawai, Nara|WD=Q1203880|lt=Kawai, Nara}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture }} {{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = Umami Kofun Cluster
(Central Cluster) | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location of the Umami Kofun Cluster (Central Cluster)}} }} '''Umami Kofun Cluster''' (馬見古墳群) is a group of Kofuns located in the southwestern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|en|Kawai, Nara|WD=Q1203880|lt=Kawai, Nara}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt=Kōryō, Nara}}, {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|en|Kashiba, Nara|WD=Q696643|lt=Kashiba}}, and {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|en|Yamatotakada, Nara|WD=Q838669|lt=Yamatotakada}} in Nara Prefecture. The cluster is situated along the Umami Hills and its surroundings and is divided into three main sections: the North, Central, and South Clusters. == Overview == The Umami Hills span approximately 7 km north-south and 3 km east-west at an altitude of 70–80 meters. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和・柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The Kofuns were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|en|Katsuragi clan|WD=Q11620908|lt=Katsuragi clan}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant Kofuns during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun|qq=}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|en|Shimanoyama Kofun|WD=Q17213138|lt=Shimanoyama Kofun}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|en|Suyama Kofun|WD=Q11479418|lt=Suyama Kofun}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samida Takarazuka Kofun|en|Samida Takarazuka Kofun|WD=Q11383079|lt=Samita Takarazuka Kofun}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|en|Otomeyama Kofun|WD=Q11369896|lt=Otomoyama Kofun}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpira Yama Kofun|lt=Konpira Yama Kofun|lt=Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpirayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsuneizuka Kofun|lt=Kitsuneizuka Kofun|lt=Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Site|1=Ikeda Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552160}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Hill Park|qq=}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|馬見古墳群|version=98265326|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:馬見古墳群]] {{wikidata link|Q11668062}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

(Central Cluster) | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location of the Umami Kofun Cluster (Central Cluster)}} }} '''Umami Kofun Cluster''' (馬見古墳群) is a group of Kofuns located in the southwestern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|en|Kawai, Nara|WD=Q1203880|lt=Kawai, Nara}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt=Kōryō, Nara}}, {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|en|Kashiba, Nara|WD=Q696643|lt=Kashiba}}, and {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|en|Yamatotakada, Nara|WD=Q838669|lt=Yamatotakada}} in Nara Prefecture. The cluster is situated along the Umami Hills and its surroundings and is divided into three main sections: the North, Central, and South Clusters. == Overview == The Umami Hills span approximately 7 km north-south and 3 km east-west at an altitude of 70–80 meters. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和・柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The Kofuns were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|en|Katsuragi clan|WD=Q11620908|lt=Katsuragi clan}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant Kofuns during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun|qq=}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|en|Shimanoyama Kofun|WD=Q17213138|lt=Shimanoyama Kofun}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|en|Suyama Kofun|WD=Q11479418|lt=Suyama Kofun}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samida Takarazuka Kofun|en|Samida Takarazuka Kofun|WD=Q11383079|lt=Samita Takarazuka Kofun}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|en|Otomeyama Kofun|WD=Q11369896|lt=Otomoyama Kofun}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpira Yama Kofun|lt=Konpira Yama Kofun|lt=Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpirayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsuneizuka Kofun|lt=Kitsuneizuka Kofun|lt=Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Site|1=Ikeda Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552160}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和・柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The Kofuns were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|en|Katsuragi clan|WD=Q11620908|lt=Katsuragi clan}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant Kofuns during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun|qq=}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|en|Shimanoyama Kofun|WD=Q17213138|lt=Shimanoyama Kofun}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|en|Suyama Kofun|WD=Q11479418|lt=Suyama Kofun}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samida Takarazuka Kofun|en|Samida Takarazuka Kofun|WD=Q11383079|lt=Samita Takarazuka Kofun}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|en|Otomeyama Kofun|WD=Q11369896|lt=Otomoyama Kofun}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpira Yama Kofun|lt=Konpira Yama Kofun|lt=Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpirayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsuneizuka Kofun|lt=Kitsuneizuka Kofun|lt=Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Site|1=Ikeda Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552160}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Hill Park|qq=}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|馬見古墳群|version=98265326|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:馬見古墳群]] {{wikidata link|Q11668062}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

(Central Cluster) | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location of the Umami Kofun Cluster (Central Cluster)}} }} '''Umami Kofun Cluster''' (馬見古墳群) is a group of Kofuns located in the southwestern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|en|Kawai, Nara|WD=Q1203880|lt=Kawai, Nara}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt=Kōryō, Nara}}, {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|en|Kashiba, Nara|WD=Q696643|lt=Kashiba}}, and {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|en|Yamatotakada, Nara|WD=Q838669|lt=Yamatotakada}} in Nara Prefecture. The cluster is situated along the Umami Hills and its surroundings and is divided into three main sections: the North, Central, and South Clusters. == Overview == The Umami Hills span approximately 7 km north-south and 3 km east-west at an altitude of 70–80 meters. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和・柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The Kofuns were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|en|Katsuragi clan|WD=Q11620908|lt=Katsuragi clan}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant Kofuns during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun|qq=}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|en|Shimanoyama Kofun|WD=Q17213138|lt=Shimanoyama Kofun}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|en|Suyama Kofun|WD=Q11479418|lt=Suyama Kofun}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samida Takarazuka Kofun|en|Samida Takarazuka Kofun|WD=Q11383079|lt=Samita Takarazuka Kofun}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|en|Otomeyama Kofun|WD=Q11369896|lt=Otomoyama Kofun}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpira Yama Kofun|lt=Konpira Yama Kofun|lt=Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpirayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsuneizuka Kofun|lt=Kitsuneizuka Kofun|lt=Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Site|1=Ikeda Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552160}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. [[ja:馬見古墳群]] [[ja:馬見古墳群]] [[ja:馬見古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}|part_of={{ill|Bakuya Kofun|WD=Q11570540|1=Bakuya Kofun}}; {{ill|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun|WD=Q17227934|1=Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun}}; {{ill|Nikiyama Kofun|WD=Q17210019|1=Nikiyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|WD=Q11369896|1=Otomeyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Samita Takarazuka Kofun|WD=Q11383079|1=Samida Takarazuka Kofun}}; {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|WD=Q17213138|1=Shimanoyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Shin-yama Kofun|WD=Q11501998|1=Shinyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Suyama Kofun|WD=Q11479418|1=Suyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun|WD=Q11603503|1=Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)}}}} {{Infobox historic site | name = Umami Kofun Cluster | native_name = 馬見古墳群 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|220px]] | image_map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}} | caption = Suyama Kofun in the Central Cluster (Special Historic Site) | type = Kofun cluster (North Cluster, Central Cluster, South Cluster) | locmapin = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|39.2|N|135|44|16.7|E|region:JP-29|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt=Kōryō, Nara|1=Kōryō, Nara}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|en|Kawai, Nara|WD=Q1203880|lt=Kawai, Nara|1=Kawai, Nara}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture }} {{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = Umami Kofun Cluster
(Central Cluster) | position = bottom | mark = Blue pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 230 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location of the Umami Kofun Cluster (Central Cluster)}} }} '''Umami Kofun Cluster''' (馬見古墳群) is a group of Kofuns located in the southwestern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|en|Kawai, Nara|WD=Q1203880|lt=Kawai, Nara|1=Kawai, Nara}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt=Kōryō, Nara|1=Kōryō, Nara}}, {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|en|Kashiba, Nara|WD=Q696643|lt=Kashiba|1=Kashiba, Nara}}, and {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|en|Yamatotakada, Nara|WD=Q838669|lt=Yamatotakada|1=Yamatotakada, Nara}} in Nara Prefecture. The cluster is situated along the Umami Hills and its surroundings and is divided into three main sections: the North, Central, and South Clusters. == Overview == The Umami Hills span approximately 7 km north-south and 3 km east-west at an altitude of 70–80 meters. The Umami Kofun Cluster contains over 250 mounds and is considered one of the "Three Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato," along with the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和・柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}.[http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) The Kofuns were constructed between the late 4th and early 6th centuries and are believed to have been the burial sites of the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|en|Katsuragi clan|WD=Q11620908|lt=Katsuragi clan|1=Katsuragi clan}}, an influential aristocratic family. The region saw the construction of significant Kofuns during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun|qq=}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|en|Shimanoyama Kofun|WD=Q17213138|lt=Shimanoyama Kofun|1=Shimanoyama Kofun}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|en|Suyama Kofun|WD=Q11479418|lt=Suyama Kofun|1=Suyama Kofun}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samida Takarazuka Kofun|en|Samida Takarazuka Kofun|WD=Q11383079|lt=Samita Takarazuka Kofun|1=Samida Takarazuka Kofun}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|en|Otomeyama Kofun|WD=Q11369896|lt=Otomoyama Kofun|1=Otomeyama Kofun}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpira Yama Kofun|lt=Konpira Yama Kofun|lt=Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpirayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsuneizuka Kofun|lt=Kitsuneizuka Kofun|lt=Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Site|1=Q11552160|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552160}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Hill Park|qq=}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|馬見古墳群|version=98265326|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:馬見古墳群]] {{wikidata link|Q11668062}} [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

The region saw the construction of significant Kofuns during the mid-Early Kofun period, including mounds exceeding 200 meters in length during the mid- to late Middle Kofun period. == Notable Kofun == The mounds are divided into three clusters based on the distribution of large keyhole-shaped kofun. === North Cluster === * {{ill|Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun|qq=}} (Kawai, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 215 m) * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|en|Shimanoyama Kofun|WD=Q17213138|lt=Shimanoyama Kofun|1=Shimanoyama Kofun}} (Kawanishi, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 190 m) * Takayamazuka 1–4 Kofun: ** Takayamazuka No. 1 (Nakayoshizuka Kofun): keyhole-shaped, total length 68 m ** Takayamazuka Nos. 2–4: three circular mounds surrounding No. 1. * Kyūzōzuka Kofun: square mound, base length 33 m, believed to be a satellite mound of Ōtsukayama Kofun. * Maruyama Kofun: scallop-shaped mound, total length 60 m. === Central Cluster === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|Restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Suyama Kofun|en|Suyama Kofun|WD=Q11479418|lt=Suyama Kofun|1=Suyama Kofun}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 204 m) - Special Historic Site. * {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kawai, Early 5th century, keyhole-shaped, total length 105 m) - Historic Site. * {{ill|Samida Takarazuka Kofun|en|Samida Takarazuka Kofun|WD=Q11383079|lt=Samita Takarazuka Kofun|1=Samida Takarazuka Kofun}} (Kawai) - Historic Site. Known for over 30 bronze mirrors, including those featuring architectural motifs. * {{ill|Shingiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 200 m). * {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|en|Otomeyama Kofun|WD=Q11369896|lt=Otomoyama Kofun|1=Otomeyama Kofun}} (Kawai and Kōryō, scallop-shaped, total length 130 m). === South Cluster === * {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kōryō, Early Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 137 m). * {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|qq=}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, Middle Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 210 m). * {{ill|Konpira Yama Kofun|lt=Konpira Yama Kofun|lt=Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpirayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). * {{ill|Kitsuneizuka Kofun|lt=Kitsuneizuka Kofun|lt=Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). * {{ill|Ikeda Site|ja|池田遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Site|1=Q11552160|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11552160}} (Yamatotakada): Contains 11 buried mounds, known as the "Ikeda Kofun Group." == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *泉森皎, "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," in {{ill|Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja|図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Sites of Japan Volume 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991. ISBN 978-4-8104-0926-0 * {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}}, "Haniwa: People and Animals" (Spring Exhibition), 2008. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Umami Hill Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Hill Park|qq=}} * {{ill|Kofun Cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/20601.htm Kofun in the Park - Umami Kofun Cluster and Umami Hill Park] (Nara Prefecture official site) * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/yamatoiseki/ |title=Yamato Archaeological Sites|date=20040609151831}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|馬見古墳群|version=98265326|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:馬見古墳群]] {{wikidata link|Q11668062}} [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
